[[co:commons:Category:Honoohonome-jinja]][[ja:火男火売神社]] '''Hinokuma Hime Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bungo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former shrine Rank was that of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}, as categorized under the modern shrine Ranking system (see {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Various Shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}). It consists of three shrines: - Upper Shrine (Oku-no-miya) located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}, - Middle Shrine (Higashiyama) on the mid-slope (also known as '''Mitake Gongen''' <おたけごんげん>), - Lower Shrine (Sato-no-miya) at the foot (also known as '''Tsurumi Gongen'''). == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Upper Shrine: {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto|ja|火之加具土命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} (ほのかぐつちのみこと), and Hiyaki Hayame no Mikoto (ひやきはやめのみこと) * Middle Shrine: Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto, and Hiyaki Hayame no Mikoto * Lower Shrine: {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}, Hinokagutsuchi no Kami (火之加具土神), and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} == History == === Founding === According to shrine tradition, the enshrinement was established in {{ill|771|ja|771|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/771|qq=}} (Hōki 2, {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=宝亀|1=Hōki}} 2). The deities that give the shrine its name, {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto|ja|火之加具土命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} and Hiyaki Hayame no Mikoto (火焼速女命), are thought to be the deification of the two peaks of {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|qid=Q1595289|lt=Sacred Mountain|1=Sacred mountains}} ({{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}) into male and female deities. It was widely known from ancient times that there were {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} at the foot of {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}'s {{ill|Hayami District, Ōita|en|Hayami District, Ōita|qid=Q1043858|lt=Hayami District|1=Hayami District, Ōita}}, but due to the active eruptions of Tsurumi-yama, the area had become barren land and marshes, and was left undeveloped. Both the {{ill|Bungo Fudoki|ja|豊後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Fudoki|qq=}} and the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}} mention features such as the "Akayu Spring" (赤湯の泉) corresponding to what is now the {{ill|Blood Pond Hell|ja|血の池地獄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blood Pond Hell|qq=}} at {{ill|Shibaishi Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaishi Onsen|qq=}}, and the "Kuberi-yu no I" (玖倍理湯の井) corresponding to the hellish area of {{ill|Tetsunawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsunawa Onsen|qq=}}. Furthermore, an excerpt from the {{ill|Iyo Fudoki|ja|伊予国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Fudoki|qq=}} recounts a {{ill|Myth|en|Myth|qid=Q12827256|lt=myth|1=Myth}} in which {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} directed a conduit from the "Hayami no Yu" ({{ill|Hayami no Yu|ja|速見の湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayami no Yu|qq=}}) spring, which emerges at the foot of Tsurumi-yama—the shrine's shintai—to {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|qid=Q1037748|lt=Dōgo Onsen|1=Dōgo Onsen}}, thereby healing the illness of {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} According to the Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku, entry of April 3, 867 (Jōgan 9)., when {{ill|867|ja|867|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867|qq=}} (Jōgan 9) on {{ill|January 20|en|January 20|qid=Q2267|lt=January 20|1=January 20}}, an eruption occurred at {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}, and at the command of the Court to the provincial governor of {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, the {{ill|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|en|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|qid=Q15905785|lt=Great Prajna Sutra|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras}} was recited before the shrine. In {{ill|927|ja|927|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/927|qq=}} (Enchō 5) the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} was established, wherein the shrine is recorded as "Hinohonome Shrine Two-seat" in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}'s {{ill|Hayami District, Ōita|en|Hayami District, Ōita|qid=Q1043858|lt=Hayami District|1=Hayami District, Ōita}}, and it is listed among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} alongside {{ill|Unagi Hime Shrine|ja|宇奈岐日女神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi Hime Shrine|qq=}} in Hayami District. In {{ill|1276|ja|1276|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1276|qq=}} (Kenji 2), the itinerant monk {{ill|Ippen|en|Ippen|qid=Q1056232|lt=Ippen|1=Ippen}}, who was traveling across Kyushu, is said to have stopped in Beppu and, guided by Tsurumi Gongen, pacified the "Kuberi-yu no I", thereby opening the stone bath (currently known as {{ill|Tetsunawa Mushiyu|ja|鉄輪むし湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsunawa Mushiyu|qq=}}) at {{ill|Tetsunawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsunawa Onsen|qq=}}. Consequently, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian deity of the {{ill|Beppu Hachiyu|ja|別府八湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu Hachiyu|qq=}}. During the Tenshō era, the shrine buildings were burned by {{ill|Otomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo Sōrin|qq=}}, and many ancient documents were lost, but the Lower Shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1664|ja|1664|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1664|qq=}} (Kanbun 4) by Kurushima Dōsei. The area around the Lower Shrine, Tsurumi Village, became an exclave of the Mori Domain, and the {{ill|Myōban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōban Onsen|qq=}} produced {{ill|Myōban|ja|明礬|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōban|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Onsen Flakes|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Flakes|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}}), which served as an important source of revenue for the domain. === Shrine Ranks === * In Kakyō 2 ({{ill|849|ja|849|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/849|qq=}}) on June 1, the deity Hinokahime was accorded the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. From the Zoku Nihon Kōki, Kakyō 2, June 1. * In Jōgan 9 ({{ill|867|ja|867|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867|qq=}}) on February 26, records indicate that the deities {{ill|Hinokami|ja|火男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokami|qq=}} and Honomai were accorded the Junior Fifth Rank, Upper and Lower respectively. From the Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku, entry of February 26, 867. * On August 16, 867 (Jōgan 9), both the deity {{ill|Hinokami|ja|火男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokami|qq=}} and the deity {{ill|Honomai|ja|火咩神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honomai|qq=}}, both of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, were jointly conferred the Senior Fifth Rank, Lower. From the Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku, entry of August 16, 867. == Cultural Properties == === Oita Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * The pagoda of the deity of the Sacred Peak at the Middle Shrine – located at the Middle Shrine. Designated on March 28, Showa 50.[http://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/education/shogaigakusyuuka/bunkazai.htm Beppu City's Designated Cultural Properties] (Beppu City Lifelong Learning Division website). === Oita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments === * The Ichiigashi forest of the Tsurumi Gongen Shrine – located at the Lower Shrine. Designated on March 28, Showa 50. * The natural forest of the deity of the Sacred Peak at the Middle Shrine – located at the Middle Shrine. Designated on March 28, Showa 50. Furthermore, the forest of Hinohonome Shrine (Tsurumi, Beppu City) was designated as a specially protected tree area in {{ill|1974|ja|1974|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49) on {{ill|March 15|en|March 15|qid=Q2403|lt=March 15|1=March 15}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/attachment/100227.pdf|title=Oita Prefecture Environmental White Paper|page=44|publisher=Oita Prefecture|accessdate=2024-09-02}} == Local Information == '''Location''' * Upper Shrine: {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}{{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|qid=Q273880|lt=Beppu City|1=Beppu}} Higashiyama – at the summit of {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}. * Middle Shrine: {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}{{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|qid=Q273880|lt=Beppu City|1=Beppu}} Higashiyama 1st District – on the mid-slope of {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}. * Lower Shrine: {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}{{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|qid=Q273880|lt=Beppu City|1=Beppu}} Honomai 8-1 – at the foot of {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}. == References == Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} (for both Middle and Lower Shrines) |founding = (Traditionally) In {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 2 ({{ill|771|ja|771|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/771|qq=}}) |main_hall = |alternative_names = |tabi = |festival = |rites = |map = {{OSM location map | coord = {{coord|33|17|9.93|N|131|25|47.17|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 260 | height = 240 | caption = | title = | minimap = file | mini-file = Map of Oita Prefecture Ja.svg | mini-width = 100 | mini-height = 100 | minipog-x = 50 | minipog-y = 41 | scalemark = 60 | shape1 = image | mark1 = Red pog.svg | label1 = Upper Shrine | mark-size1 = 6 | mark-coord1 = {{coord|33|17|9.93|N|131|25|47.17|E}} | label-pos1 = top | label-size1 = 12 |label-color1 = black |mark-description1= Hinohonome Shrine Upper | mark2 = Red pog.svg | label2 = Middle Shrine | mark-size2 = 6 | mark-coord2 = {{coord|33|16|28.16|N|131|26|14.18|E}} | label-pos2 = bottom | label-size2 = 12 |label-color2 = black |mark-description2= Hinohonome Shrine Middle | mark3 = Red pog.svg | label3 = Lower Shrine | mark-size3 = 6 | mark-coord3 = {{coord|33|18|34.83|N|131|27|59.38|E}} | label-pos3 = top | label-size3 = 12 |label-color3 = black |mark-description3= Hinohonome Shrine Lower }} }} {{Commonscat|Honoohonome-jinja}} [[File:火男火女神社1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Hinohonome Shrine''' (火男火売神社, also '''Hinohonome Shrine''' (火男火賣神社)) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|qid=Q273880|lt=Beppu City|1=Beppu}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former shrine Rank was that of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}, as categorized under the modern shrine Ranking system (see {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Various Shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}). It consists of three shrines: - Upper Shrine (Oku-no-miya) located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}, - Middle Shrine (Higashiyama) on the mid-slope (also known as '''Mitake Gongen''' <おたけごんげん>), - Lower Shrine (Sato-no-miya) at the foot (also known as '''Tsurumi Gongen'''). == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * Upper Shrine: {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto|ja|火之加具土命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} (ほのかぐつちのみこと), and Hiyaki Hayame no Mikoto (ひやきはやめのみこと) * Middle Shrine: Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto, and Hiyaki Hayame no Mikoto * Lower Shrine: {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}, Hinokagutsuchi no Kami (火之加具土神), and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} == History == === Founding === According to shrine tradition, the enshrinement was established in {{ill|771|ja|771|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/771|qq=}} (Hōki 2, {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=宝亀|1=Hōki}} 2). The deities that give the shrine its name, {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto|ja|火之加具土命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} and Hiyaki Hayame no Mikoto (火焼速女命), are thought to be the deification of the two peaks of {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|qid=Q1595289|lt=Sacred Mountain|1=Sacred mountains}} ({{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}) into male and female deities. It was widely known from ancient times that there were {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} at the foot of {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}'s {{ill|Hayami District, Ōita|en|Hayami District, Ōita|qid=Q1043858|lt=Hayami District|1=Hayami District, Ōita}}, but due to the active eruptions of Tsurumi-yama, the area had become barren land and marshes, and was left undeveloped. Both the {{ill|Bungo Fudoki|ja|豊後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Fudoki|qq=}} and the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}} mention features such as the "Akayu Spring" (赤湯の泉) corresponding to what is now the {{ill|Blood Pond Hell|ja|血の池地獄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blood Pond Hell|qq=}} at {{ill|Shibaishi Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaishi Onsen|qq=}}, and the "Kuberi-yu no I" (玖倍理湯の井) corresponding to the hellish area of {{ill|Tetsunawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsunawa Onsen|qq=}}. Furthermore, an excerpt from the {{ill|Iyo Fudoki|ja|伊予国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Fudoki|qq=}} recounts a {{ill|Myth|en|Myth|qid=Q12827256|lt=myth|1=Myth}} in which {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} directed a conduit from the "Hayami no Yu" ({{ill|Hayami no Yu|ja|速見の湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayami no Yu|qq=}}) spring, which emerges at the foot of Tsurumi-yama—the shrine's shintai—to {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|qid=Q1037748|lt=Dōgo Onsen|1=Dōgo Onsen}}, thereby healing the illness of {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} According to the Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku, entry of April 3, 867 (Jōgan 9)., when {{ill|867|ja|867|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867|qq=}} (Jōgan 9) on {{ill|January 20|en|January 20|qid=Q2267|lt=January 20|1=January 20}}, an eruption occurred at {{ill|Mount Tsurumi|en|Mount Tsurumi|qid=Q6924219|lt=Tsurumi-yama|1=Mount Tsurumi}}, and at the command of the Court to the provincial governor of {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, the {{ill|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|en|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|qid=Q15905785|lt=Great Prajna Sutra|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras}} was recited before the shrine. In {{ill|927|ja|927|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/927|qq=}} (Enchō 5) the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} was established, wherein the shrine is recorded as "Hinohonome Shrine Two-seat" in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}'s {{ill|Hayami District, Ōita|en|Hayami District, Ōita|qid=Q1043858|lt=Hayami District|1=Hayami District, Ōita}}, and it is listed among the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} alongside {{ill|Unagi Hime Shrine|ja|宇奈岐日女神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unagi Hime Shrine|qq=}} in Hayami District. In {{ill|1276|ja|1276|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1276|qq=}} (Kenji 2), the itinerant monk {{ill|Ippen|en|Ippen|qid=Q1056232|lt=Ippen|1=Ippen}}, who was traveling across Kyushu, is said to have stopped in Beppu and, guided by Tsurumi Gongen, pacified the "Kuberi-yu no I", thereby opening the stone bath (currently known as {{ill|Tetsunawa Mushiyu|ja|鉄輪むし湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsunawa Mushiyu|qq=}}) at {{ill|Tetsunawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsunawa Onsen|qq=}}. Consequently, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian deity of the {{ill|Beppu Hachiyu|ja|別府八湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu Hachiyu|qq=}}. During the Tenshō era, the shrine buildings were burned by {{ill|Otomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo Sōrin|qq=}}, and many ancient documents were lost, but the Lower Shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1664|ja|1664|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1664|qq=}} (Kanbun 4) by Kurushima Dōsei. The area around the Lower Shrine, Tsurumi Village, became an exclave of the Mori Domain, and the {{ill|Myōban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōban Onsen|qq=}} produced {{ill|Myōban|ja|明礬|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōban|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Onsen Flakes|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Flakes|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}}), which served as an important source of revenue for the domain. === Shrine Ranks === * In Kakyō 2 ({{ill|849|ja|849|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/849|qq=}}) on June 1, the deity Hinokahime was accorded the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. From the Zoku Nihon Kōki, Kakyō 2, June 1. * In Jōgan 9 ({{ill|867|ja|867|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867|qq=}}) on February 26, records indicate that the deities {{ill|Hinokami|ja|火男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokami|qq=}} and Honomai were accorded the Junior Fifth Rank, Upper and Lower respectively. From the Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku, entry of February 26, 867. * On August 16, 867 (Jōgan 9), both the deity {{ill|Hinokami|ja|火男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokami|qq=}} and the deity {{ill|Honomai|ja|火咩神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honomai|qq=}}, both of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, were jointly conferred the Senior Fifth Rank, Lower. From the Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku, entry of August 16, 867. == Cultural Properties == === Oita Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * The pagoda of the deity of the Sacred Peak at the Middle Shrine – located at the Middle Shrine. Designated on March 28, Showa 50.[http://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/education/shogaigakusyuuka/bunkazai.htm Beppu City's Designated Cultural Properties] (Beppu City Lifelong Learning Division website). === Oita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments === * The Ichiigashi forest of the Tsurumi Gongen Shrine – located at the Lower Shrine. Designated on March 28, Showa 50. * The natural forest of the deity of the Sacred Peak at the Middle Shrine – located at the Middle Shrine. Designated on March 28, Showa 50. Furthermore, the forest of Hinohonome Shrine (Tsurumi, Beppu City) was designated as a specially protected tree area in {{ill|1974|ja|1974|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49) on {{ill|March 15|en|March 15|qid=Q2403|lt=March 15|1=March 15}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.oita.jp/uploaded/attachment/100227.pdf|title=Oita Prefecture Environmental White Lower shrine haiden | location = Upper shrine: {{ill|lt=Beppu|Q273880|qid=Q273880|1=Beppu}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|qid=Q133924|1=Ōita Prefecture}} (Mt. Tsurumi summit)
Middle shrine: Higashiyama 1-ku, Beppu
Lower shrine: Hiuri 8-1, Beppu | coordinates = Upper shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|17|9.93|N|131|25|47.17|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=Honoohonome Shrine Upper}}
Middle shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|16|28.16|N|131|26|14.18|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=Honoohonome Shrine Middle}}
Lower shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|18|34.83|N|131|27|59.38|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=Honoohonome Shrine Lower}} | deity = Different by shrine section | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (two seats)
former {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=kensha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} (middle and lower shrines) | founded = (trad.) 771 }} {{Commonscat|Honoohonome-jinja}} [[File:火男火女神社1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Honoohonome Shrine''' (火男火売神社, also written 火男火賣神社) is a Shinto shrine complex in {{ill|lt=Beppu|Q273880|qid=Q273880|1=Beppu}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|qid=Q133924|1=Ōita Prefecture}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; under the modern system, its middle and lower shrines were prefectural shrines. The complex consists of three shrines on {{ill|lt=Mount Tsurumi|Q6924219|qid=Q6924219|1=Mount Tsurumi}}: the upper shrine (okumiya) at the summit, the middle shrine ("Ontake Gongen") on the mountainside, and the lower shrine (satomiya, "Tsurumi Gongen") at the foot. === Enshrined deities === * Upper shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}, Hiyaki-hayame-no-Mikoto * Middle shrine: Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto, Hiyaki-hayame-no-Mikoto * Lower shrine: {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami, {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === History === === Foundation === Shrine tradition states the foundation was in 771 (Hoki 2). The paired deities in the shrine name, Hi-no-Kagutsuchi and Hiyaki-hayame, are interpreted as deifications of the male and female peaks of Mt. Tsurumi, a sacred mountain. The area had well-known hot springs since ancient times, but volcanic activity left much terrain difficult to use. Old texts such as the ''Bungo Fudoki'' and ''Manyoshu'' mention nearby geothermal areas; an ''Iyo Fudoki'' fragment also preserves a myth that "Hayami no Yu" from the Tsurumi foothills was connected to {{ill|lt=Dogo Onsen|Q1037748|qid=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}} to heal Sukunahikona. === Historical outline === The ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' records that when Mt. Tsurumi erupted in 867, the court ordered the reading of the Great Prajna Sutra before this shrine. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Honoohonome Shrine, two seats" in Hayami District, Bungo Province, and is listed with {{ill|lt=Unaguhime Shrine|Q11449252|qid=Q11449252|1=Unaguhime Shrine}} as a district shikinaisha. In 1276, {{ill|lt=Ippen|Q1056232|qid=Q1056232|1=Ippen}} is said to have visited Beppu and, by guidance of Tsurumi Gongen, pacified "Kuberi no Yu-no-i" and opened a stone steam bath in Kannawa (today's {{ill|lt=Kannawa Mushi-yu|Q11649634|qid=Q11649634|1=Kannawa Steam Bath}}). The shrine came to be revered as a guardian of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Beppu Hatto|1=UNKNOWN}} hot springs. In the Tensho era, shrine buildings and documents were burned during actions by Otomo Sorin. In 1664, Kurushima Michikiyo rebuilt the lower shrine. === Court rank (Shinkai) === * 849-06-01: Honoho-me deity granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower * 867-02-26: Honoo deity listed as Junior Fifth Rank, Upper; Honome deity as Junior Fifth Rank, Lower * 867-08-16: both raised to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower === Cultural properties === === Oita Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Tahoto pagoda of Ontake Gongen shrine (middle shrine) - designated March 28, 1975.[http://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/education/shogaigakusyuuka/bunkazai.htm Beppu City designated cultural properties] (Beppu City Lifelong Learning Division). === Oita Prefecture designated natural monuments === * Ichiigashi forest of Tsurumi Gongen shrine (lower shrine) - designated March 28, 1975. * Natural forest of Ontake Gongen shrine (middle shrine) - designated March 28, 1975. The shrine forest (Beppu, Tsurumi) was also designated a special protected forest on March 15, 1974. === Site information === '''Locations''' * Upper shrine: summit of Mt. Tsurumi, Higashiyama, Beppu * Middle shrine: mountainside of Mt. Tsurumi, Higashiyama 1-ku, Beppu * Lower shrine: Hiuri 8-1, foothills of Mt. Tsurumi, Beppu === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''': introduced for source tracing. {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Related literature === {{座標一覧}} * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1050845762582671744 Sakuo Abe, "On Honoohonome Shrine: Guardian deity of Beppu hot springs"] (CiNii) {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hinokuma Hime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hinokuma Hime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hinokuma Hime Shrine]][[Category:onsen shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Oita Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Beppu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Beppu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]][[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]]
